name: Life Fitness
notes: Founded 1977 in Chicago. Acquired from Brunswick by KPS Capital Partners for $490M in 2019. Brand family includes Hammer Strength, Cybex, SCIFIT, and ICG. Largest market share in US gym equipment manufacturing.

name: Hammer Strength
notes: Founded 1989. Acquired by Life Fitness in 1997. Industry standard for commercial plate-loaded and selectorized strength equipment. Found in most commercial gyms.

name: Cybex
notes: Founded 1970 in Medway, MA. Acquired by Brunswick/Life Fitness for $195M in 2016. Known for Arc Trainer elliptical and premium commercial cardio/strength lines.

name: SCIFIT
notes: Founded 1987 in Tulsa, OK. Specializes in medical rehabilitation and accessible fitness equipment. Serves hospitals, PT clinics, senior living. Engineering and assembly in Tulsa.

name: Technogym
notes: Founded 1983 in Cesena, Italy. Publicly traded (BIT: TGYM). Official supplier to 9 Olympic Games. Opened new US HQ in Jersey City, NJ in March 2025. Over 100,000 facilities worldwide. Premium home and commercial lines.

name: Precor
notes: Founded 1980 in Woodinville, WA. Acquired by Peloton for $420M in 2021. Invented the elliptical fitness crosstrainer (EFX). Now integrated into Peloton's Commercial Business Unit. US manufacturing in Washington state.

name: Peloton
notes: Founded 2012 in NYC. Publicly traded (NASDAQ: PTON). Revenue ~$2.44B (TTM Dec 2025). Owns Precor. Products: Peloton Bike, Bike+, Tread, Tread+, Row. Launching Commercial Series for gyms in 2026. CEO Peter Stern (Jan 2025).

name: NordicTrack
notes: Founded 1975 in Minnesota. Acquired by ICON (now iFIT) in 1998. Flagship brand of iFIT. Known for incline trainers, iFIT interactive platform. Launched Ultra 1 treadmill for 50th anniversary (2025). HQ: Logan, UT.

name: ProForm
notes: Founded 1987. Subsidiary of iFIT (formerly ICON Health & Fitness). Mass-market brand with iFIT interactive technology. Award-winning connected equipment including treadmills, ellipticals, cycles, climbers.

name: Freemotion
notes: Commercial brand of iFIT. Pioneer of cable-based strength training, Incline Trainer, first road-simulating indoor bike. High-end commercial and connected fitness equipment.

name: Weider
notes: Brand owned by iFIT (formerly ICON). Focuses on weight-based strength-training equipment including home gyms and smith machines. Budget-oriented brand sold primarily through mass retailers.

name: Weslo
notes: Entry-level brand owned by iFIT (formerly ICON). Known for Cadence series treadmills. Lowest price tier in the iFIT brand family. Basic no-frills equipment.

name: Gold's Gym (Home Equipment)
notes: Licensed brand. iFIT (formerly ICON) has manufactured Gold's Gym home fitness equipment under license since 2001. Includes treadmills, ellipticals, home gyms. Not to be confused with Gold's Gym franchise gyms.

name: Matrix Fitness
notes: Launched 2001. Commercial/premium brand of Johnson Health Tech (Taiwan). Equipment for health clubs, luxury resorts, athletic teams. Expanded to home market in 2016. JHT generated $962M revenue.

name: Horizon Fitness
notes: Based in Cottage Grove, WI. Home/consumer brand of Johnson Health Tech. Known for quality mid-range home cardio equipment. Studio series indoor cycles.

name: Vision Fitness
notes: Light commercial and residential brand of Johnson Health Tech. Domestic and commercial equipment. Sold through specialty dealer network.

name: Bowflex
notes: Originally part of Nautilus Inc, rebranded to BowFlex Inc in 2023. Filed bankruptcy March 2024. Acquired by Johnson Health Tech for $37.5M in April 2024. Known for SelectTech dumbbells, Max Trainer, home gyms with rod-based resistance.

name: Schwinn Fitness (Home)
notes: Home fitness brand. Was part of Nautilus/BowFlex Inc. Acquired by Johnson Health Tech in 2024 bankruptcy sale. Popular models: IC4/IC8 indoor cycles, 270/230 recumbent bikes. All models under $1000.

name: Schwinn (Commercial)
notes: Commercial Schwinn cycling products (AC Power, Z Bike, IC Classic) are sold by Core Health & Fitness for studio/gym use. Separate from home Schwinn brand now owned by JHT.

name: Nautilus (Commercial)
notes: Commercial Nautilus brand sold through Core Health & Fitness. Nautilus invented the modern selectorized strength machine. Commercial assets sold to Core Health & Fitness in 2014. Widely considered the founder of the modern commercial gym.

name: StairMaster
notes: Invented the stepper category. Sold by Nautilus to Core Health & Fitness in 2009. Flagship 10G model features industry's widest/deepest steps and OverDrive Training Mode. Next Gen lineup debuted at HFA 2025.

name: Star Trac
notes: Commercial cardio brand under Core Health & Fitness. Known for Spinner brand indoor cycling bikes. Next Gen 8 & 10 Series cardio debuted 2025. Core H&F revenue ~$75M.

name: Sole Fitness
notes: Based in Salt Lake City, UT. Owned by Dyaco International (Taiwan). Known for F80, F85, F63 treadmills. Popular mid-range brand with strong dealer presence. F85 sold exclusively at Dick's.

name: Spirit Fitness
notes: Brand of Dyaco International (Taiwan, founded 1989). Commercial and residential lines. Dyaco revenue ~$216M (2025). Known for reliable mid-range commercial and home equipment.

name: XTERRA Fitness
notes: Mass-market brand of Dyaco. Based in Jonesboro, AR. Manufactured in Taiwan. Equipment priced $350-$1000. Lifetime frame warranty. Sold at Costco and major retailers. Entry-level alternative to Sole.

name: True Fitness
notes: Founded 1981 in St. Louis, MO. Privately held, CEO Frank Trulaske. Acquired Octane Fitness in 2020 for $25M. Premium commercial and residential fitness equipment. Exhibited at FIBO 2025.

name: Octane Fitness
notes: Founded 2005. Originally part of Nautilus Inc. Sold by Nautilus to True Fitness for $25M in October 2020. Known for zero-runner, lateral elliptical (LateralX), and cross-circuit training.

name: Inspire Fitness
notes: HQ in Corona, CA with presence in Xiamen, China. Over 20 years experience. Merged with Centr wellness platform in 2022. Known for FT2 and SF3 functional trainers, smith machines. Premium-engineered home and commercial strength equipment.

name: Body-Solid
notes: Founded 1989 in Chicago, IL. HQ in Forest Park, IL (200,000+ sq ft facility). Over 500 product SKUs. Known for lifetime 'Built for Life' warranty. Serves home, commercial, military, rehab markets. A+ BBB rating.

name: Rogue Fitness
notes: Founded 2006 in Columbus, OH. Revenue ~$510M (2025). ~1,000 employees. Official equipment supplier of CrossFit Games and Arnold Strongman. US-manufactured racks, barbells, plates. Premium strength and conditioning equipment.

name: REP Fitness
notes: Founded 2012 by Ryan and Shane McGrotty in Longmont, CO. HQ in Westminster, CO. Expanded R&D facility in 2025. Home and commercial strength equipment: racks, benches, functional trainers, barbells, cable machines. Strong value proposition.

name: Titan Fitness
notes: Founded 2005 in Memphis, TN by Russell Robinson. Part of Titan Brands (nearly 3,000 products across fitness, outdoor, tractor). Budget alternative to Rogue. Known for affordable power racks, cages, benches, and accessories.

name: Force USA
notes: American-designed, sold in 24+ countries over 20 years. Invented the All-In-One Trainer concept. C20 model: world's first All-In-One with 3x3 uprights, freestyle pulleys. Sliding Bench named 'Most Innovative Weight Bench 2025' by Garage Gym Reviews.

name: Marcy
notes: Manufactured by Impex Inc., founded 1982 in City of Industry, CA. American family-owned. Brands: Marcy, Steelbody, Bionic Body. Known for affordable home gyms, smith machines, weight benches. Long-standing budget fitness brand.

name: Sunny Health & Fitness
notes: Founded 2002 by John Sun. HQ: City of Industry, CA. Started with 12 products and a van. Now 71+ employees. Leading budget fitness brand on Amazon. Distributor/importer model. Strong e-commerce presence.

name: Concept2
notes: Founded 1976 in Morrisville, VT by Dick and Pete Dreissigacker. Gold standard for indoor rowing machines (Model D/E, RowErg). Air resistance. Also makes BikeErg and SkiErg. Used in CrossFit, competitive rowing, Olympics. 39+ dealers worldwide.

name: WaterRower
notes: Founded 1988 in Warren, RI by John Duke (Yale engineer). Water resistance flywheel design replicates rowing on water. Handcrafted wood construction as 'fitness furniture'. Also owns NOHrD brand. Americas HQ in Warren, RI.

name: Hydrow
notes: Founded 2017 in Cambridge, MA. ~300 employees. Connected rowing machine with HD touchscreen and live/on-demand classes. Electromagnetic resistance. Models: Hydrow, Wave, Origin. Private company, growing rapidly.

name: Keiser
notes: Founded 1976 in Fresno, CA by Dennis Keiser. Pioneer of pneumatic (air-powered) resistance training. M3i indoor cycle is industry standard for cycling studios. Known for M Series bikes with magnetic resistance and power meter. Bluetooth data transmission.

name: Hoist Fitness
notes: HQ in Poway, CA. Revenue ~$35M (2025). ~60 employees across 3 continents. Premium home and commercial strength equipment. Known for V-Series selectorized, ROC-IT line, and multi-gyms.

name: TuffStuff Fitness
notes: HQ in Chino, CA. Acquired by Brooks Industrial Marketplace in December 2022. ~51 employees. Known for commercial-grade multi-gym systems, functional trainers, and smith machines. Cal Gym sub-brand.

name: Bodycraft
notes: Founded 1994. Known for innovative home gym designs: EXP series, functional trainers, SPX indoor cycles, VR500 rowing machine. Lifetime in-home manufacturer warranty on all equipment. Residential and light commercial.

name: Landice
notes: Founded 1967 in Randolph, NJ. Second-generation family business. One of the few manufacturers assembling in the USA (98% US-sourced parts). Known for L7, L8 treadmills and L890 rehab treadmill. 40+ years of premium quality.

name: Woodway
notes: Originally founded 1975 in Germany (Weil am Rhein). US operations in Waukesha, WI since 1988. Patented slat-belt technology reduces impact and provides natural running feel. Largest specialized treadmill manufacturer in the world. Customers: universities, hospitals, pro sports teams.

name: SportsArt
notes: Taiwan-based, 40+ years in business. Sold in 80+ countries. 500,000+ sq ft manufacturing. Pioneer of ECO-POWR technology: converts up to 74% of human energy into electricity (up to 220 Wh/hour). ISO-14001 certified since 2011. BWH Hotel Group supplier.

name: Assault Fitness
notes: Founded 2004. Originally a division of LifeCORE Fitness (Carlsbad, CA). Acquired by FitLab in February 2024. Known for AssaultBike (air bike), AssaultRower, AssaultRunner (curved treadmill). Standard equipment for CrossFit, military, pro sports.

name: Diamondback Fitness
notes: Built on 40+ years of Diamondback cycling expertise. Sister brand to Assault Fitness under LifeCORE. Makes recumbent bikes, upright bikes, indoor cycles, ellipticals. Product lineup significantly reduced in recent years -- possible transition period.

name: Echelon
notes: HQ in Chattanooga, TN. ~246 employees. Connected fitness equipment with streaming classes. Buyout/LBO by Forte (FitLab) in July 2025. Originally positioned as affordable Peloton alternative. Spin bikes, treadmills, rowers, mirrors.

name: MYX Fitness
notes: Acquired by Beachbody (now BODi) in June 2021 via SPAC merger. Connected indoor cycling bikes (MYX II). BODi trades on NASDAQ under 'BODI'. Beachbody streaming content integrated.

name: YOSUDA
notes: Founded 2018 by Eric Zeng. Based in Los Angeles, CA with manufacturing in Shenzhen, China. Owns factories and R&D. Best-selling budget exercise bike on Amazon. Magnetic resistance, LCD monitors. Known for affordability.

name: JOROTO
notes: Founded 1982 in Colorado, USA. Subsidiaries in US, UK, France, Australia, China. Sold in 90+ countries, 28 product categories. Premium home fitness focus. Over 3,000 brand stores worldwide. Magnetic resistance bikes are top sellers on Amazon.

name: Exerpeutic
notes: Brand of Paradigm Health & Wellness Inc., founded 2007 in City of Industry, CA. Revenue ~$6.9M (2025). Known for folding exercise bikes and compact equipment for small spaces. Budget-oriented.

name: ProGear
notes: Brand of Paradigm Health & Wellness Inc. Budget manual and motorized treadmills, under-desk bikes. Steel frame construction. Sister brand to Exerpeutic and Fitness Reality.

name: LifeSpan Fitness
notes: Launched 2002, based in Salt Lake City, UT. Pioneer of under-desk treadmill/walking pad category for workplace wellness. Known for TR1200, TR5000, TX6 models. Also makes Ampera bike desk that generates electricity. 25+ years in fitness.

name: Tonal
notes: Founded 2015 in San Francisco. Wall-mounted digital weight system with electromagnetic resistance up to 200 lbs. New CEO Krystal Zell (April 2025). Received $130M funding. AI-powered coaching and adaptive resistance. Connected fitness pioneer.

name: Pooboo
notes: Chinese brand with 20 years in fitness equipment. Served 1,000,000+ families. Specializes in affordable home gym equipment. Strong Amazon presence. Exercise bikes, ellipticals, rowing machines.

name: MERACH
notes: Leading smart home fitness brand from Asia. Debuted FantomFite immersive fitness game at CES 2025. Products include NovaWalk W50 walking pad, NovaBike S60 Pro with Bluetooth. Aggressive pricing with rapid feature iteration.

name: Torque Fitness
notes: Founded 2005 by team with experience dating to 1979. Award-winning innovative strength training systems. Home and commercial functional trainers, plate-loaded equipment, storage solutions. Minnesota-based.

name: Nautilus (Home)
notes: Home Nautilus brand was part of BowFlex Inc. (formerly Nautilus Inc). Acquired by Johnson Health Tech in April 2024 bankruptcy sale along with Bowflex and Schwinn. Performance-oriented home cardio equipment.

name: HealthRider
notes: Brand owned by iFIT (formerly ICON Health & Fitness). Home fitness equipment. Budget tier alongside Weslo and Gold's Gym in the iFIT brand portfolio.

name: Core Health & Fitness
notes: Parent company operating StairMaster, Star Trac, Nautilus (commercial), Schwinn (commercial), and Throwdown brands. Revenue ~$75M (2025). ~370 employees across 6 continents. World's 5th largest commercial fitness equipment company.

name: Johnson Health Tech
notes: HQ in Taichung, Taiwan. Largest fitness equipment manufacturer by revenue ($962M). Brands: Matrix, Horizon, Vision, Bowflex, Schwinn. 600+ products. Number-one specialty fitness retailer worldwide. Acquired BowFlex Inc brands in 2024 for $37.5M.

name: iFIT Health & Fitness
notes: Formerly ICON Health & Fitness, rebranded 2021. HQ in Logan, UT. Brands: NordicTrack, ProForm, Freemotion, Weider, Weslo, Gold's Gym (licensed), HealthRider. World's largest home fitness equipment supplier. Settled class action lawsuits 2024-2025.

name: Impulse Health Tech
notes: Based in Qingdao, China. Major global commercial fitness equipment manufacturer. Full industrial chain from R&D to gym operation. Selected into 'Top 500 Chinese Manufacturing Enterprises' 2025. Sold worldwide.
